skill.name: react-flow-architect
skill.description: Expert ReactFlow architect for building interactive graph applications with hierarchical node-edge systems, performance optimization, and auto-layout integration.
skill.summary: Build production-ready ReactFlow applications with hierarchical navigation, memoization, and advanced state management patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 110 potential issues but all are false positives. The skill contains documentation and TypeScript code examples for ReactFlow graph visualization. No executable code, external commands, cryptographic operations, or system access patterns exist. The 31 external_commands findings reference code snippets in documentation, not actual shell execution. The 79 cryptographic algorithm findings have no basis - no crypto code exists in this visualization skill.

content.user_title: Build Interactive Graph Applications with ReactFlow
content.value_statement: Creating complex node-based graph visualizations requires managing hierarchical data, performance optimization, and state synchronization. This skill provides proven patterns for building production-ready ReactFlow applications with efficient rendering and advanced navigation.
actual_capabilities: Build hierarchical tree structures with expandable and collapsible nodes
Implement performance optimization using React.memo and useMemo for large graphs
Create auto-layout integration with Dagre for automatic node positioning
Manage complex state with undo/redo history and persistence patterns
Implement focus mode to isolate and highlight connected nodes
Add search functionality with automatic navigation to matching nodes
limitations: Requires ReactFlow library and React environment setup
Performance patterns assume graphs with 1000+ nodes need virtualization
Auto-layout integration requires Dagre library dependency
State management patterns use reducer pattern requiring TypeScript knowledge

use_cases.title: Workflow Diagram Builder
use_cases.description: Create interactive workflow diagrams where users can expand process categories, view step details, and navigate through connected workflows with smooth transitions.
use_cases.target_user: Process engineers designing business workflow documentation

use_cases.title: System Architecture Visualizer
use_cases.description: Build system architecture diagrams with hierarchical component views, allowing teams to explore microservice dependencies and data flow connections interactively.
use_cases.target_user: Software architects documenting distributed system designs

use_cases.title: Data Pipeline Explorer
use_cases.description: Visualize data pipelines with nodes representing transformations and edges showing data flow, enabling users to trace data lineage and identify bottlenecks.
use_cases.target_user: Data engineers monitoring ETL pipeline health and dependencies

prompt_templates.title: Basic Graph Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a basic ReactFlow graph component with 5 nodes and connecting edges. Include proper TypeScript types for Node and Edge interfaces with position data and labels.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with ReactFlow for simple node visualization

prompt_templates.title: Hierarchical Tree with Expansion
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a hierarchical tree structure where nodes can be expanded to show children. Implement the TreeNode interface with level, hasChildren, isExpanded, and category properties. Include the buildVisibleNodes function for incremental rendering.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating expandable organization charts or file system trees

prompt_templates.title: Performance Optimization Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement the useIncrementalGraph hook with useMemo-based change detection. Compare expanded list changes versus other dependencies to enable incremental updates. Include caching for visible nodes and edges.
prompt_templates.scenario: Optimizing graphs with 500+ nodes to maintain 60fps rendering

prompt_templates.title: Auto-Layout with Dagre Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create the applyLayout function using Dagre for automatic node positioning. Configure rankdir, nodesep, and ranksep options. Implement debounced layout calculation with caching to prevent redundant computations during rapid changes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Automatically arranging complex graphs without manual node positioning

output_examples.input: Create a basic ReactFlow graph with three connected nodes representing a simple workflow
output_examples.output: A complete ReactFlow component with properly typed nodes (id, position, data.label) and edges (id, source, target). The graph displays three workflow steps connected sequentially with interactive selection capability.

output_examples.input: Add expand/collapse functionality to a hierarchical node tree
output_examples.output: Implementation of the buildVisibleNodes function with recursive child discovery, expandedIds Set management, and incremental rendering. Parent nodes display child count indicators and toggle buttons for expansion control.

best_practices: Use React.memo for custom node components with custom comparison functions to prevent unnecessary re-renders when only selected state changes
Implement debounced layout calculations with 150ms delay to avoid expensive Dagre computations during rapid user interactions
Cache layout results using Map with generated cache keys to restore previous layouts without recalculation when returning to known graph states
anti_patterns: Calling layout functions directly in render without memoization causes expensive recalculations on every state change
Using array.find for node lookups in hot paths instead of Map-based O(1) access creates performance bottlenecks with large graphs
Storing expanded state as array instead of Set causes O(n) lookup times when checking visibility during recursive tree traversal

faq.question: How many nodes can ReactFlow handle before performance degrades?
faq.answer: ReactFlow can handle 500+ nodes with proper memoization. For 1000+ nodes, implement virtualization and incremental rendering patterns from this skill. The useIncrementalGraph hook reduces render time by only updating changed portions.

faq.question: Do I need Dagre for basic graph layouts?
faq.answer: No, Dagre is optional for automatic layouts. You can manually position nodes using the position property. Dagre integration is recommended for complex graphs where automatic hierarchical arrangement saves time.

faq.question: How do I implement undo/redo for graph changes?
faq.answer: Use the history management pattern with a GraphState containing a history array and historyIndex. Save state snapshots on changes and navigate the history array on undo/redo actions through the reducer.

faq.question: Can I customize node appearance beyond the default styling?
faq.answer: Yes, create custom node components using React.memo. Pass your component via the nodeTypes prop in ReactFlow. Custom nodes receive data, selected, and other props for full styling control.

faq.question: How do I focus on a specific node and its connections?
faq.answer: Use the useFocusMode hook pattern that filters nodes and edges based on the selected node. It identifies direct connections and dims unrelated elements with opacity and blur transitions.

faq.question: What TypeScript types do I need for ReactFlow nodes?
faq.answer: Extend the base Node type with your data interface. For hierarchical trees, include level, hasChildren, isExpanded, childCount, and category properties in the data object for proper type safety.
